transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
*   The patient denies blood in stool.
*   The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
*   The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
*   The patient reports chest pain when walking.
*   The patient describes the chest pain as a burning sensation.
*   The patient reports experiencing the chest pain approximately three times per week.
*   The patient reports endorsing nasal congestion due to allergies.
*   The clinician hears a slight three out of six systolic ejection murmur.
*   The echocardiogram shows a slightly low pumping function of the heart at 45%.
*   The echocardiogram shows mitral regurgitation.
*   The ECG shows stable findings reflecting prior coronary artery disease.
*   The clinician plans to continue the patient on aspirin.
*   The clinician plans to start the patient on toprol.
*   The clinician orders a lipid panel.
*   The clinician orders a cardiac stress test.
source_block:
- The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
- The patient denies blood in stool.
- The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
- The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
- The patient reports chest pain when walking.
- The patient describes the chest pain as a burning sensation.
- The patient reports experiencing the chest pain approximately three times per week.
- The patient reports endorsing nasal congestion due to allergies.
- The clinician hears a slight three out of six systolic ejection murmur.
- The echocardiogram shows a slightly low pumping function of the heart at 45%.
- The echocardiogram shows mitral regurgitation.
- The ECG shows stable findings reflecting prior coronary artery disease.
- The clinician plans to continue the patient on aspirin.
- The clinician plans to start the patient on toprol.
- The clinician orders a lipid panel.
- The clinician orders a cardiac stress test.
